stories.title: HSBC’s Jain on Hormuz Strait Reopening
stories.summary: Parash Jain, Global Head of Transport and Logistics at HSBC, says shipping companies remain in “wait-and-see” mode as the Strait of Hormuz edges closer to reopening under a US–Iran agreement. He notes the next 40–50 days will be critical in determining whether more vessels can pass through, as de-mining operations are still underway. He does not expect significant movement in the short term. He sp

stories.title: Financial Details Emerge as US, Iran Prepare for Signing
stories.summary: The US and Iran are preparing to formally sign an interim peace deal, with details of the accord still emerging and energy insiders skeptical of how fast it can reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg Economics' Chris Kennedy breaks down the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.summary: "Balance of Power: Late Edition" focuses on the intersection of politics and global business. On today's show, Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-MA), says she is optimistic Congress can pass bipartisan housing legislation and get it to President Trump's desk. stories.title: Sen. Johnson: US Must Keep Iran ‘In a Box’
stories.summary: Senator Ron Johnson (R-WI), a member of the Senate Homeland Security and Finance Committees, says he has not seen the full Iran memorandum of understanding and remains skeptical Tehran can be trusted, while arguing the US should keep monitoring Iran and retain the option of further strikes if it moves toward its nuclear program. He also defends President Trump’s energy policy and says he is genera

stories.title: Anthropic's Fable shutdown is a big moment for open-source AI
stories.summary: Anthropic’s Fable shutdown could boost open-source AI, but many of the models gaining traction are Chinese.

stories.title: Brookfield-Backed Data Center Firm Csquare Files for US IPO
stories.summary: Csquare Inc., a data center company backed by Brookfield Corp., filed publicly for a US initial public offering, joining a rush of debuts linked to artificial intelligence infrastructure.

stories.title: Oil Holds Near Three-Month Low as Iran Deal Seen Boosting Supply
stories.summary: Oil held near a three-month low on expectations that a US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz will unleash a wave of supply.

stories.title: Senator Warren on Iran Deal, Fed's Warsh and SpaceX IPO
stories.summary: US Senator Elizabeth Warren, a Massachusetts Democrat, says President Donald Trump "can't point to where we're better off" after the war in Iran. Speaking on "Balance of Power: Evening Edition," Senator Warren also discusses Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's military budget request, her criticisms of Kevin Warsh as chair of the Federal Reserve, the state of housing and the SpaceX IPO. (Source: Bloo

stories.title: AI Can Play 'Significant' Role in Tech for Managing Diabetes, Says David Roman
stories.summary: David Roman, US medtech and healthcare IT research analyst for Goldman Sachs, said that AI play a 'significant' role in helping patients stay on top of their diabetes, with companies like Abbott and Dexcom using new technology to count carbohydrates and deliver the right amount of insulin. Roman, joined by Bloomberg health reporter Madison Muller, said that he came away from Goldman Sach's annual

stories.title: Bond Options Traders Split on Fed Rate Path as Warsh Era Begins
stories.summary: Options traders are increasingly divided over the Federal Reserve’s near-term rate path, with conflicting bets that span from cuts to various degrees of hikes over the coming months.

stories.title: Kevin Warsh Makes His Fed Debut With Trump Pushing for Rate Cuts
stories.summary: Serious changes are likely coming to the central bank.

stories.title: Binance Prepares for EU Exit With Local License in Limbo
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Anna Irrera joins Scarlet Fu and Tim Stenovec on "Bloomberg Crypto." Binance is making preparations to stop providing services in the European Union, as a deadline to receive a license to operate in the bloc draws near and the crypto exchange has yet to receive approval from Greece’s financial regulator. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Treasury yields slide as Fed begins monetary policy meeting
stories.summary: U.S. Treasury yields fell on Tuesday as the Federal Reserve's two-day policy meeting kicked off.

stories.title: Australia Must Gird for Shock-Prone Financial System, RBA Warns
stories.summary: Australian institutions need to be ready to respond to a more shock-prone financial system as a strained geopolitical environment reshapes financial and economic linkages, a senior Reserve Bank official said Wednesday.

stories.title: Read the 14-Point Draft Memorandum Between the US and Iran
stories.summary: The US and Iran are expected to formally sign a memorandum of understanding on June 19 in Switzerland, paving the way for 60 days of talks aimed at ending their war for good and putting strict new limits on Iran’s nuclear program.

stories.title: Starmer Touts £1.3 Billion of Energy, AI Investment at G7 Summit
stories.summary: Keir Starmer announced £1.3 billion of investment into the UK in energy and AI projects at the G7 summit in France, as Britain’s premier tries to convey a sense of business as usual even as he faces the prospect of a leadership challenge.

stories.title: How the Iran War Is Leaving Lasting Scars Across Asia
stories.summary: On today’s Big Take Asia podcast, we discuss how war in Iran hit Asia especially hard and why structural economic problems are likely to persist, even with a peace deal in the works.

stories.title: Amazon Faces Billions in Penalties From Potential FTC Ad Suit
stories.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is facing a possible lawsuit from the US Federal Trade Commission that may lead to billions of dollars in civil penalties, over claims the e-commerce giant misled advertisers, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.title: Seeking Crypto Policy 'Clarity' in DC

stories.title: BlackRock Launches New Bitcoin Income ETF
stories.summary: Robert Mitchnick, global head of digital assets at BlackRock, joins Scarlet Fu and Tim Stenovec on "Bloomberg Crypto." BlackRock launched today the iShares Bitcoin Premium Income ETF (Ticker: BITA). (Source: Bloomberg)

Bloomberg's Anthony Hughes and Mike Dinsdale, CEO at Powerlaw join to discuss Powerlaw’s platform allowing exposure to private

stories.title: Pope Leo Hopes US-Iran Peace Deal Will Be Definite End to War
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV welcomed an agreement between Iran and the US to reach a peace deal, expressing hope it will provide a definitive end to the war.

stories.title: Fed Chair Warsh expected to withhold 'dot' from central bank's interest rate outlook
stories.summary: The central bank's Federal Open Market Committee is set to release its quarterly update of where individual officials expect interest rates to head.

stories.title: Lutnick’s Letter to Anthropic Warned of Curbs on Top AI Models
stories.summary: US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick warned Anthropic PBC in a letter last week that it would need government permission to grant foreign nationals access to its most advanced AI models and threatened criminal and civil penalties if the firm failed to comply, according to a copy seen by Bloomberg News.

stories.title: Read the Lutnick Letter That Led Anthropic to Disable Mythos
stories.summary: US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick warned Anthropic PBC Chief Executive Officer Dario Amodei in a letter Friday that the company would need government permission before exporting its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 artificial intelligence models to any destination worldwide, or to any foreign national regardless of location.

stories.title: SpaceX Soars On Third Trading Day, Seals Cursor Takeover | Bloomberg Tech 6/16/2026
stories.summary: Bloomberg’s Ed Ludlow tracks SpaceX's value eclipsing Amazon's, this as it lands its $60 billion takeover of Cursor. Plus, Anthropic meets with US officials to resolve a national security dispute with the Trump administration over its most advanced AI models. And, Sequoia Partner Shaun Maguire talks about the AI landscape and why he never wants to sell his SpaceX shares. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Can Hong Kong make a giant leap to commercial space insurance?

In reality, space, which can feel lofty and far away to pragmatic Hongkongers, could hold the key to our city’s economic future. stories.title: HSBC Expects Over $100 Million Gains From Using Google AI Tools

stories.summary: The decision comes after a prolonged period of difficulty for the chain, which has faced increasing competition from a range of rivals.

stories.title: Exxon to Supply LNG to Help South Africa Reduce Reliance on Coal

stories.title: Private-Credit Defaults Match 2023 High in a KBRA Index
